Integrate design system tokens (#87)

This pull request introduces a new dark mode design system, "Obsidian
Metric Dark," for the frontend, along with detailed implementation
guardrails to ensure consistency with the Stitch system. It also adds
export manifests and documentation for the design system and related UI
assets. The changes emphasize strict visual and implementation rules for
frontend development, focusing on tonal layering, typography, and
component behavior.

**Frontend Implementation Policy and Guardrails**
- Added a comprehensive section to `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
outlining mandatory frontend implementation rules under the Stitch
system, including source of truth locations, required visual rules (such
as tonal layering, typography, and card structure), validation steps for
UI changes, and guidance to prioritize Stitch over legacy defaults.

**Design System Assets and Documentation**
- Introduced a Stitch export manifest (`EXPORT_MANIFEST.md`) listing the
exported design system assets, including dark and light variants, and
associated dashboard screens.
- Added the "Obsidian Metric Dark" design system JSON
(`15dd6710f8154a37b4e9b80acc4494f8.design-system.json`), defining the
theme, color palette, typography, elevation, and component
specifications for the new dark mode.
- Provided a detailed Markdown specification for the "Obsidian Metric
Dark" design system
(`15dd6710f8154a37b4e9b80acc4494f8.design-system.md`), describing the
visual philosophy, color rules, typography, depth, components, and
signature interactions.

Redesign chat customizations welcome page (#308622)

* Redesign chat customizations welcome page

- Extract welcome page into dedicated AICustomizationWelcomePage class
  and aiCustomizationWelcome.css for better code organization
- Replace old banner + card grid with:
  - 'Analyze Your Project and Configure AI' input box that opens
    /agent-customization in chat with user's description
  - 2x3 category card grid (Agents, Skills, Instructions, Hooks,
    MCP Servers, Plugins) with actionable chip buttons
  - Chips prepopulate chat with specific prompts (e.g. 'Create a
    code review agent', 'Enforce coding style')
- Remove redundant 'Chat Customizations' heading (already in tab)
- Remove redundant 'browse by type' pills (cards serve same purpose)
- Improve chip contrast with visible borders and link-colored text
- Tighten card padding and description sizing for cleaner layout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Polish welcome page: bigger input, separator, more copy

- Make input box taller with rounded corners and inner submit button
  styled like a chat input (pill-shaped submit that fills on hover)
- Add section divider line between input area and card grid
- Add 'Or configure individual customizations:' label above grid
- Expand description copy to mention codebase analysis
- Bump card label font-weight to 600 and restore 12px desc size
- Restore card padding to 14px for better breathing room

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rework welcome page: stacked list, better input, centered divider

- Replace 2x3 card grid with full-width stacked category list
  where each row shows icon, label, description, and chip buttons
  inline on a single line
- Input box uses cornerRadius-large, proper padding matching chat
  input style, submit button inside with pill shape
- Separator now shows centered text: 'or configure individually'
  with lines extending on both sides
- Reduce max-width to 680px and remove all excess whitespace
- Remove card/grid CSS entirely

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve welcome page hierarchy and command styling

- Restore strong page intro with 'Chat Customizations' heading and
  descriptive copy from the original welcome page
- Increase overall scale: wider content, larger workflow heading,
  larger description text, and roomier input box
- Break each section into title row, description row, and command row
  instead of compressing everything into a single line
- Restyle example actions as monospace command-like entries such as
  '/create-skill ...' to better communicate they prepopulate chat
- Make category rows roomier with card-like boundaries and better
  visual hierarchy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify welcome page into launcher-style sections

- Replace boxed customization cards with lightweight stacked sections
  separated by subtle rules
- Keep the centered 'or configure individually' separator
- Increase overall scale for the intro, workflow heading, copy, and input
- Make command examples look like real slash commands with plain
  command-link styling instead of pill buttons
- Separate each section into title, description, and command rows for
  clearer hierarchy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert welcome page to original design (keep file separation)

Restore the original welcome page layout from main exactly as it was:
- 'Chat Customizations' heading + subtitle
- 'Configure Your AI' getting-started banner
- Responsive card grid with Browse and Generate with AI buttons

The only structural change kept is the file separation:
welcome page logic lives in aiCustomizationWelcomePage.ts and
welcome CSS lives in media/aiCustomizationWelcome.css.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add centered 'or configure individually' divider between banner and cards

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Configure Your AI banner with chat-style input box

Swap the old getting-started banner for a clean input box styled
like the chat input part: cornerRadius-large, input-background,
submit arrow inside. No highlight borders or extra UI chrome.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix input box focus outlines to match chat input part

Remove the double-outline: the inner monaco-inputbox and the native
input element both had their own focus borders showing through. Force
border/outline to none on both inner elements and let only the outer
container show the single focus border. Use transparent as default
border (matching chat input) and add overflow: hidden to clip inner
outlines at the rounded corners.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix inner input box border by passing transparent inputBoxStyles

The InputBox sets an inline style border via applyStyles() which
cannot be overridden by CSS. Pass inputBorder: transparent and
inputBackground: transparent so the inner box is invisible and
only the outer container provides the border.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Split the Agent Customizations welcome page into classic and prompt-launcher variants, add a configuration switch for selecting the implementation, and add component fixtures for the welcome page variants.

Move the polished workflow-box layout into promptLaunchers (now the default) and restore classic to the origin/main baseline. Enable the getting-started banner in sessions. Fix double-box input styling and card footer alignment.

Replace InputBox widget with plain <input> element to eliminate the focus border that the global .monaco-workbench input[type=text]:focus rule was applying. Move helper text above the input, match input-row rounding to the outer card, and add strong specificity overrides for all focus states.

feat(research): EXP-1571 robustness archetype characterization

Classifies 11 patients into robustness tiers based on how their
meal-clock regularity responds to detection parameter changes:

commit.message:
Fix: Support both old and new metadata schemas in step3

The step2_download_era5_parallel.py script was recently updated to use 'first_time'
and 'last_time' columns instead of 'start_time' and 'end_time' (commit 5f9f8f3).

However, step3_precompute_composites.py was still hardcoded to expect 'start_time',
causing KeyError: 'start_time' when processing data files created with the new
step2 schema while trying to get the case month for climatology lookups.

This fix:
1. Creates _get_case_start_time() helper function to handle both schemas
2. Supports backward compatibility with old metadata files (start_time)
3. Supports new metadata files (first_time)
4. Provides clear error messages if neither column is found

This allows step3 to process mixed old/new metadata files on servers that may
have both legacy and newly-generated data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): re-enable Maglev execution with dead arguments elimination

Re-add replace_dead_arguments() optimizer pass that replaces unused
CreateMapped/UnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter with UndefinedConstant
in-place. This preserves graph structure (node count, positions) while
eliminating the expensive trampoline call.

Re-enable try_execute_maglev() which was disabled because every sloppy-mode
function triggered unconditional deopt via CreateMappedArguments. With dead
arguments elimination, most functions no longer have this node and can
execute successfully in Maglev.

Expected impact: ~70-800x improvement on all 9 benchmarks (interpreter-only
was 58-5982µs, Maglev was 0.7-3.0µs for the working benchmarks).

Fix PDB Symbol Resolution for Unmerged Windows Traces (#2407)

* Fix PDB symbol resolution for unmerged Windows traces

The ELF symbol work introduced a switch on BinaryFormat in
LookupSymbolsForModule that only handled PE and ELF cases. For
unmerged Windows traces, symbolInfo is null (no RSDS events in the
ETL), so BinaryFormat returns Unspecified, causing symbol resolution
to be skipped entirely.

Add a case for ModuleBinaryFormat.Unspecified that falls back to PDB
lookup on Windows. OpenPdbForModuleFile handles missing PDB signatures
gracefully by checking the local file on disk.

Also rename ModuleBinaryFormat.Unknown to Unspecified to better reflect
that the format was not specified in the trace data.

[build] Use PolySharp for C# 13 support on legacy TFMs (#3642)

[build] Use PolySharp for C# 13 support on legacy TFMs (#3642)

SkiaSharp still ships net462 and netstandard2.x targets, so newer C# language
features need the compiler support attributes that newer frameworks already
provide. Add PolySharp as a private build-time dependency for those legacy
TFMs and update the compatibility package versions used there.

Because PolySharp embeds the required System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis and
System.Runtime.CompilerServices support types into the final assemblies, the
repo-local NullableAttributes.generated.cs shim and its build registration are
no longer needed and can be removed.

Keep the dependency metadata aligned with the final package set by recording the
updated System.Memory and System.Runtime.CompilerServices.Unsafe versions in
VERSIONS.txt and the docs dependency scan. Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces was
evaluated during the work, but removed after validating that the legacy
SkiaSharp builds still succeed without it.

refactor(codegen): thread explicit type hints into collection builtins (#887)

* refactor(codegen): thread typeHint into generateBuiltinCall for Vec/HashMap/HashSet::new

Pass pendingDeclaredType.value_or(mlir::Type{}) from the call-dispatch
site in MLIRGenExpr.cpp into generateBuiltinCall, and use the explicit
typeHint parameter instead of reading/resetting the member inside the
three collection builtins (Vec::new, HashMap::new, HashSet::new).

All other pendingDeclaredType consumers (Some/Ok/Err/None/array/map/
block-literal) are unchanged. Stmt-level set/reset in MLIRGenStmt.cpp
is unchanged. This is a pure refactor slice with no semantic change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(codegen): clear builtin type hint before sibling lowering

Capture and clear pendingDeclaredType at the builtin-call dispatch site before
delegating to generateBuiltinCall, so explicit collection constructor hints
cannot leak into sibling subexpressions evaluated during the same RHS.

Add a focused mlirgen regression test covering Vec::new() adjacent to an array
literal in the same expression.

household-ID wiring PR. The createViewModel() helper was already updated
but these three tests bypassed it:

- clearSelection resets selected account and transactions

repository.description: Trackly ist eine SSR-first Zeiterfassung (PHP 8.2 + MariaDB) für kleine Vereine: manuelle Zeiten & Timer, regelbasierte Flags (Arbeitsrecht), Audit-Logs, Rückfragen/Koordination, Ankündigungen, sowie CSV/PDF-Export (wkhtmltopdf). Fokus: RBAC, CSRF, PRG, deterministische Tests & idempotente Migrations/Seeds.

commit.message:
Refine CI workflow: limit push triggers and gate coverage reports (#393)

* fix: enable coverage report posting to PRs

The vitest-coverage-report-action requires pull_request event context to
post comments. The workflow only triggered on push events, so the action
had no PR to comment on. Add pull_request trigger and guard the report
step with an event check.
